title: Chicken Bao

ingredients.title: Bao Dough (Fluffy Mantou-style Bun)
items: 5 g (1 tsp) baking powder (for extra fluffiness)

ingredients.title: Chicken Filling (Umami-rich, aromatic)
items: 400 g boneless chicken thighs, finely diced (or minced)
50 g napa cabbage, finely chopped, lightly salted, excess water squeezed

instruction.title: Make Bao Dough
steps: Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water. Rest 5 minutes until foamy.
In a mixer bowl, combine flour, yeast mixture, oil, salt. Knead 8–10 min until smooth.
Cover and proof in warm area 60–90 min until doubled.
Punch down, knead briefly, rest 10 min.
Roll into log, divide into 12–14 equal pieces, shape into balls. Cover to prevent drying.

instruction.title: Prepare Chicken Filling
steps: Mix all filling ingredients in a bowl until tacky (stir in one direction for texture).
Chill mixture for 20–30 minutes for easier handling.

instruction.title: Assembly
steps: Roll each dough ball into a 9–10 cm circle (slightly thicker in center).
Pleat edges evenly and twist to seal (classic baozi fold).
Place each bao on parchment square.

instruction.title: Steaming
steps: Prepare a bamboo steamer lined with parchment or cabbage leaves.
Turn off heat, rest 2 min before opening lid (prevents collapse).

instruction.title: Presentation
steps: Serve hot, garnished with a brush of sesame oil for shine.
Plate with pickled cucumbers or a microgreen garnish.
Optional dip: Black vinegar + thinly sliced ginger.

chefTips: Use chicken thigh for succulence. Breast dries out.
Always proof twice: first bulk ferment, then shaped proof.
Control steaming heat – medium-high prevents bursting.
